questions.questionText:
The interior angle of a regular polygon is five times its exterior angle.
Find the number of sides of the polygon.

markScheme.desc: Interior + exterior = 180°, and interior = 5 × exterior, so 6 × exterior = 180°

workingSteps: Let exterior angle = x°. Then interior angle = 5x°.
questions.explanation: Interior and exterior angles are supplementary; exterior angles sum to 360°.

questions.questionText:
Lines AB and CD are parallel. EF is a transversal.
Angle AEF = (3x + 10)° and angle EFD = (5x − 30)°.
Find the value of x and state which angle relationship you used.
workingSteps: AEF and EFD are alternate angles (AB ∥ CD)
questions.explanation: Alternate angles between parallel lines are equal.

questions.context: ABCDEF is a regular hexagon with centre O.

parts.label: a
workingSteps: Angle at centre = 360° ÷ number of sides

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Show that triangle AOB is equilateral.
parts.correctAnswer: OA = OB = AB (all equal to radius); angle AOB = 60°, so all angles are 60°

markScheme.desc: OA = OB = radius of circumscribed circle
Triangle is isosceles with apex 60°, so base angles = (180 − 60)/2 = 60°; all angles 60°, equilateral

workingSteps: OA = OB (both radii of the circumscribed circle)
Triangle OAB is isosceles, so base angles = (180° − 60°)/2 = 60°
All three angles are 60°, so all sides are equal: OA = OB = AB

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Hence find the interior angle of the regular hexagon.

markScheme.desc: Interior angle = angle FAB = angle OAB + angle OAF = 60° + 60°

workingSteps: By symmetry, angle OAF = 60° (triangle AOF is also equilateral)
